title: '26 minute nap' — Oct 2019 title-only note
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only Apple Note from Oct 28, 2019, 5:45am — Yahir is 17, senior year at Grants Pass High. Title "26 minute nap" is the entire artifact; body is empty.
  • The exact six-second gap between created and modified timestamps (5:45:52 → 5:45:58) shows this was a single capture, never returned to — the canonical note, where the title is the note.
  • The pre-dawn timestamp + the suspiciously-precise "26 minute" duration suggests a polyphasic-sleep experiment or post-late-night-study micro-recovery — the kind of teen self-optimization that bookends the Aug 2019 simplicity manifesto season.
  • Sits in the same fall-2019 cluster as the "money means nothing" aphorism and the coding zeal note — Yahir oscillating between life-philosophy declarations and granular self-quantification in the same week.
  • The note is a clean specimen of the title-as-data, body-as-silence pattern — the duration logged but never analyzed, the act-of-noting itself the only output.

Source

"26 minute nap"

Why it matters in the graph

  • A representative datapoint for the sub-pattern title-only-with-empty-body — the form Yahir uses when the act of recording is more important than the content. Worth a recurring tag for grep.

Open questions

  • Was 26 minutes the actual nap or the target? Most polyphasic protocols cite 20- or 26-minute "NASA naps" specifically.
  • Did Yahir track other naps this week? No companion notes found in the corpus — single datapoint.
